system.description: A fleet of pirate ships, each with its own captain, bound by a strict charter written by the First Captains. Ships flying the Brotherhood flag offer quarter to any crew that surrenders without a fight — and ruin to those who don't. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: The Brotherhood primarily concerns itself with gold. Politics rarely interest it, and the machinations of the nations of Théah matter only when large amounts of plunder are involved.
system.earnfavor:
• Selling information that leads to a ship the Brotherhood can plunder earns an additional Favor (3 Favor total). The Brotherhood will also pay 1 Wealth from the ship's spoils.
  • Aiding an Agent of the Brotherhood in capturing a prize earns two shares of the prize (typically at least 2 Wealth) in addition to the normal Favor gains.
system.callupon:
• Smuggle items or someone into or out of a secured area, past an enemy blockade, or similar for 4 Favor. The Brotherhood delivers it to a reasonable safe port; once it leaves the ship, it's no longer their problem.
  • Blockade a sea pass with Brotherhood ships for 7 Favor (approximately a week, longer if the plunder proves worthwhile).

system.description: The Crusaders — a five-centuries-old order of Eisen knights, branded traitors by the Inquisition and vanished into the shadows, who now hunt the monsters and dark magic that ordinary Théans cannot face. Their symbol is a white star on a black field. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: Die Kreuzritter primarily concerns itself with inhuman monsters and dark magic — a monster's weakness, proof that a Baron is secretly a necromancer, or the location of a blade that turns the walking dead to dust.
system.earnfavor:
• Selling information about a monster, a dark sorcerer, or a cursed or dark artifact earns 2 additional Favor (4 total) after the creature is destroyed, the sorcerer defeated, or the artifact claimed or destroyed by Die Kreuzritter agents.
  • Acquiring dracheneisen gains you 10 Favor — the most effective weapon against Monsters.
system.callupon:
• Access to a reliquary costs 4 Favor: the loan of a relic (typically a dracheneisen weapon) for a single mission, which must be returned or the agent is declared a traitor.
  • A dracheneisen weapon's location costs 9 Favor, granting the special "Dracheneisen Seeker" Story (at least 10 Steps) that rewards a dracheneisen blade upon completion.

system.description: Founded by the Castillian scholar Leandra Souza to stop "diggers" from looting ancient ruins, the Society leads Théah's archæological research — cataloguing Syrneth artifacts and keeping the largest library of maps in the world. Members wear their silver compass pins openly. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: The Explorer's Society primarily concerns itself with the ancient ruins of Numa and Syrneth, and any texts, notes, or artifacts from such places. It craves knowledge of the past to better the future.
system.earnfavor:
• Acquiring a relic or lost knowledge is worth 4 Favor, if that knowledge or relic is turned over or shared with the Society for study.
  • Finding a Syrneth or Numanari ruin and then informing the Society is worth 5 Favor.
system.callupon:
• Access to a reliquary costs 3 Favor: a rare relic that functions like a Signature Item (effects at GM discretion) and must be returned once the mission is complete.
  • Access to an Explorer Society dig costs 2 Favor (you may be on site, but probably cannot remove relics).

system.description: Hidden scholars who continue the Vaticine Church's scientific work in secret, dodging High Inquisitor Verdugo's ban on all research. Members know each other only by pseudonyms, each aware of only two others, passing data through an underground network. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: The Invisible College primarily concerns itself with gathering and transferring information to others, with scientific discovery, and with restoring the Church to its former glory.
system.earnfavor:
• Selling information or secrets is always worth at least 1 additional Favor if it is related to the Inquisition, their activities, or their agents.
  • Saving important scientific knowledge from destruction is worth 6 Favor if that knowledge is then shared with the Invisible College.
system.callupon:
• Passing a message or small package through the College's information network costs 1 Favor — it can reliably reach almost anywhere in Théah.
  • Buying a dangerous secret costs 5 Favor (an advanced gunpowder formula, a potent poison and its antidote, the location of a controversial hidden tome).

name: The Knights of the Rose & Cross

system.description: Théah's strangest secret society — because it isn't secret at all. A famous "gentle's society" of knights devoted to justice and protecting the defenseless, with open chapter houses across Théah. Actual Knights serve three years of dangerous duty; wealthy supporters join as "Benefactors." Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: The Knights of the Rose & Cross primarily concern themselves with acts of derring-do, protecting those who cannot protect themselves, and acting as a moral example for others.
system.earnfavor:
• Recruiting a new Benefactor to the Rose & Cross is worth 4 Favor (a candidate must have a reputation for responsibility and caring for those under their protection).
  • Going on a heroic Quest (a Story the Order requests) earns, in addition to normal Story rewards, Favor equal to the number of Steps in the Story.
system.callupon: • Financial or material support from a Benefactor costs 1 Favor per 1 Wealth point. Large sums (more than 5 Wealth) draw attention and may require additional service to keep the Benefactor safe.

system.description: A masked order born of Castillian patriots, sworn to protect the "goodly crowns" of Théah and overthrow corrupt ones. There is no single El Vagabundo — five agents share five masks of great power, each of which steals the wearer's identity. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: Los Vagabundos are vigilantes, concerned with injustice regardless of who the victim is. Remember that injustice is not always the same as crime — robbing a corrupt man may be just.
system.earnfavor:
• Saving a Heroic noble from a Villain is worth 4 Favor.
  • Defeating a Villainous noble is worth 8 Favor.
system.callupon:
• Donning the Mask costs 10 Favor: while you wear the Mask of El Vagabundo you gain 1 additional Rank in all Traits and all Skills, but you cannot activate your Virtue or gain Hero Points from your Hubris or Quirks (you may still gain Hero Points from other sources).
  • Requesting the aid of an agent costs 3 Favor. An agent of Los Vagabundos is always +2 Strength more than an agent of another Society.

system.description: Curonian for "grandmother's shawl" — a movement with no hierarchy, no officers, and no dues that began aiding war widows and orphans, and now runs toward every disaster with doctors, food, and warm blankets. Its only requirement is compassion. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: Močiutės Skara primarily concerns itself with war and disaster relief — anywhere people suffer, go hungry, or have no clean water — and, increasingly, with stopping wars before they begin.
system.earnfavor:
• Providing food, water, or medical relief to a community in need is worth 5 Favor. The Shawl cares nothing for borders, wars, or nobility.
  • Preventing an army from going to battle is worth 10 Favor, by any means — stealing its provisions, negotiating a treaty, or kidnapping the general.
system.callupon: • Purchase information, request an agent, or purchase a secret from any other Society. The Shawl is uniquely on good terms with every Society, giving it the best support network in Théah (this does not grant access to restricted materials, such as a dracheneisen blade).

system.description: The Freethinkers — a leaderless, deliberately chaotic order founded by the legendary "Uppman," waging "mimeme warfare" against the Two Great Tyrannies of religion and monarchy. Their creed: "No Gods, No Kings." They gather in hands of no more than five. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: Rilasciare primarily concerns itself with authority and tyranny — specifically, destroying both. They envision a Théah free of kings, nobility, the church, and every other master.
system.earnfavor:
• Overthrowing a noble ruler is worth 6 Favor.
  • Thwarting a Villainous Church official is worth 4 Favor (they realize there are good souls within the Church's hierarchy).
system.callupon:
• Gathering a Strength 10 Brute Squad (a Rilasciare Mob of armed commoners) costs 3 Favor; for each additional 3 Favor its Strength increases by 10. Once loosed on a rough instruction, the Hero loses all control over it.
  • Naming someone as Uppman's Friend costs 2 Favor (6 Favor for a noble or church official): they gain the Rilasciare's protection and are immune from its punitive actions.

system.description: The Vodacce sect of the Rilasciare, begun as a conspiracy between a Merchant Prince's wife and his courtesan to overthrow his power. Almost exclusively female, they run reading circles, smuggle Sorte Strega out of Vodacce, and shield those sympathetic to their cause. Membership costs nothing but loyalty; a Hero may belong to only one Secret Society.
system.concern: Sophia's Daughters primarily concern themselves with protecting and smuggling fate witches out of Vodacce, and with supporting female authority in other Nations.
system.earnfavor:
• Smuggling a Fate Witch out of Vodacce is worth 10 Favor — you must get the Sorte Strega across the borders to a safe haven.
  • Undermining a Merchant Prince's plot is worth 6 Favor (turning two Princes against one another, all the better).
system.callupon:
• Requesting aid from a Fate Witch costs 3 Favor. She is typically Strength 6, possesses the Sorcery (Sorte) Advantage, and rolls 2 additional dice in any social Risk involving Vodacce courtly politics.
  • Naming someone as Sophia's Friend works the same as Naming someone as Uppman's Friend, at the same costs (2 Favor, or 6 for a noble or church official).
